rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type contracts in component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use only valid visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Use appropriate scope for each type of AGREE contract
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current scope
Follow the principle of least privilege when selecting visible identifiers
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equations, `const` for constants, `assume` for assumptions, and `guarantee` for guarantees
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type contracts when concrete bounds are available
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants
Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid scopes
